independent_source.note: The frozen value is the block-internal (little-endian) merkle root that OpenTimestamps commits to; it is the byte-reverse of the big-endian value block explorers display. Fetched once, offline-verifiable forever against any Bitcoin block header for height 957504.

anchor._wp_a1_note: WP-A1: bitcoinBlockHeaderMerkleRootsByHeight under rpTrust is RELYING-PARTY trust (independently sourced from independent_source below); frozen is kept as producer evidence only and is NEVER trusted. A confirmed verdict requires the relying-party header.

rationale: The only externally confirmed end-to-end OpenTimestamps case in the corpus: the OTS proof commits to the decision content root (16b80b4c…) and carries a BitcoinBlockHeaderAttestation at height 957504, whose real block merkle root (independently fetched from blockstream.info) matches the proof's committed root — verified offline against the frozen merkle root, never by contacting a calendar. The corpus runs the positive check; that a WRONG frozen root is rejected (block_mismatch, so confirmation is not a blind pass) is exercised by tests/test_anchors_ots.py. This case proves the anchor lifecycle and cross-implementation canonicalization; it does NOT prove decision-receipt/v0.1 schema conformance — the predicate reports 12 findings (expected-fail). The field mapping is in audit_artifacts/crossimpl_fixture_gap_20260711.md (that record analyses the regenerated 3abe69f vector, decision root ff05e3e0…; this OLD 9ab2ed2 vector, decision root 16b80b4c…, carries the same predicate shape and the 12-finding count is reproduced here).
